title: Fixes: status filters for podman ps and podman pod ps should be inclusive
body:
Signed-off-by: zhangguanzhang <zhangguanzhang@qq.com>
Fixes: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/8344#issuecomment-728830411

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan*, *zhangguanzhang* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Also doing more testing with docker some filters appear to be inclusive (status) while other are exclusive (label). I need to see if I find something where this is documented.

> Also doing more testing with docker some filters appear to be inclusive (status) while other are exclusive (label). I need to see if I find something where this is documented.

got it

The docker logic is quite complex: https://github.com/moby/moby/blob/5c10ea6ae80589c2d56d618df54927e92b0b0136/daemon/list.go#L410

In general filters with the same key are inclusive, e.g. `--filter name=con1 --filter name=con2` returns con1 and con2. The only exception to this seems to be `label` which is exclusive.
Filters with different keys are exclusive, e.g. `--filter name=con1 --filter status=created` will only return con1 if it has the status created.
I took a look, this logic is too complicated for me🙄。May need someone else to implement this feature
